title: How Well Do You Know Christianity?
description: Thirty questions across theology, church history, Christian living, church and ministry.

questions.question: What does the doctrine of the Trinity teach?
options: God is one person appearing in three forms
The one God eternally exists as Father, Son and Holy Spirit
Jesus became divine after the resurrection
questions.explanation: Christian teaching confesses one God in three distinct persons: Father, Son and Holy Spirit.

questions.question: What does the incarnation refer to?
options: The Son of God becoming truly human
The church becoming a political power
questions.explanation: The incarnation is the Christian claim that the eternal Son took on human nature in Jesus Christ.

questions.question: Which event stands at the centre of Christian hope?
questions.explanation: Jesus’ resurrection is the beginning of new creation and the foundation of Christian hope.

questions.question: What is the gospel?
options: Good news about God’s saving work through Jesus Christ
questions.explanation: The gospel is good news about what God has done through Jesus’ life, death, resurrection and reign.

questions.question: What is sanctification?
options: The Spirit’s continuing work of forming believers into Christlikeness
The act of translating the Bible
questions.explanation: Sanctification describes the ongoing transformation of believers in holiness and Christlike character.

questions.question: What is a sacrament or ordinance?
options: A political law enforced by the church
A practice such as baptism or the Lord’s Supper commanded by Christ
questions.explanation: Christians use these terms for practices instituted by Christ, especially baptism and the Lord’s Supper.

questions.question: What was the main issue at the Council of Nicaea in AD 325?
options: Which English Bible should be used
questions.explanation: Nicaea defended the full divinity of the Son against Arian teaching.

questions.question: What did the Protestant Reformation especially challenge?
options: The existence of the Old Testament
Abuses and doctrines within the medieval Western church
questions.explanation: Reformers challenged abuses and argued for renewal in doctrine, worship and church authority.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Christian prayer?
options: Communication with God shaped by worship, confession, thanksgiving and request
questions.explanation: Christian prayer is relational communion with God and includes worship, confession, thanksgiving and petition.

questions.question: What is discipleship?
options: Learning to follow Jesus in belief, character and practice
questions.explanation: Discipleship is the lifelong process of learning from Jesus and living under his lordship.

questions.question: What is the church?
options: The community of people called to belong to Christ
questions.explanation: In the New Testament, the church is primarily the gathered and scattered people of God.

questions.question: What does repentance involve?
options: Turning towards God with changed allegiance and behaviour
questions.explanation: Repentance is a turning of mind, loyalty and life towards God.

questions.question: What is grace?
options: God’s unearned favour and generous action
questions.explanation: Grace is God’s undeserved generosity, most fully revealed in salvation through Christ.

questions.question: What is meant by the authority of Scripture?
options: Scripture functions as a trustworthy norm for Christian faith and life
Only clergy may read the Bible
The Bible answers every modern question directly
questions.explanation: Christians affirm Scripture as authoritative while still needing careful interpretation.
questions.link_text: Read How to Interpret the Bible

questions.question: What is the Christian hope for creation?
options: The renewal of creation under God’s reign
The destruction of all material existence
questions.explanation: The biblical hope is resurrection and renewed creation, not merely escape from the world.

questions.question: Which virtue is included in the fruit of the Spirit?
questions.explanation: Galatians 5 includes self-control among the fruit produced by the Spirit.

questions.question: Why do Christians gather for worship?
options: To honour God, hear Scripture, pray, share fellowship and be formed as Christ’s people
To avoid all contact with society
questions.explanation: Christian worship gathers believers around God’s word, prayer, praise, fellowship and mission.

questions.question: What does forgiveness require?
options: Releasing personal vengeance while still taking truth and safety seriously
questions.explanation: Forgiveness rejects revenge but does not erase truth, accountability or wise boundaries.

questions.question: What is Christian mission?
options: Participating in God’s work of witness, service, reconciliation and disciple-making
questions.explanation: Mission includes proclaiming Christ, making disciples, serving others and bearing witness to God’s kingdom.

questions.question: What does justification describe?
options: God declaring sinners righteous through Christ
questions.explanation: Justification refers to God’s gracious declaration that sinners are put right with him through Christ.

questions.question: Who were the apostles?
options: Authorised witnesses and messengers of Jesus
questions.explanation: The apostles were commissioned witnesses of Jesus, especially his resurrection and teaching.
questions.link_text: Read How We Got the Bible

questions.question: What does catholic mean in the phrase 'the catholic church' in early creeds?
questions.explanation: In the creeds, catholic means universal, referring to the whole church across places and times.

questions.question: What is Christian hospitality?
options: Welcoming and caring for others, including strangers
questions.explanation: Biblical hospitality is generous welcome, especially towards those who may not be able to repay it.

questions.question: What is the purpose of spiritual gifts?
options: Building up the church and serving others
questions.explanation: The New Testament presents spiritual gifts as graces given for service and the common good.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Christian leadership?
options: Servant leadership shaped by Christ’s example
Recognition of the most gifted speaker
questions.explanation: Christian leadership should be accountable, sacrificial and directed towards serving and equipping others.

questions.question: What is lament?
options: Honest prayer expressing grief, protest and trust before God
questions.explanation: Biblical lament brings pain honestly to God while continuing to seek him.

questions.question: What did the early creeds mainly seek to do?
options: Summarise and safeguard central Christian beliefs
questions.explanation: Creeds condensed core convictions about God, Christ, the Spirit, the church and resurrection.

questions.question: Why is love of neighbour central to Christian ethics?
options: Jesus joined it closely to love for God
It applies only to church members
It removes the need for truth
questions.explanation: Jesus named love of God and neighbour as the greatest commandments.

questions.question: What is pastoral care?
options: Supporting people spiritually, emotionally and practically
Providing professional therapy in every situation
questions.explanation: Pastoral care attends to people with prayer, listening, guidance, presence and appropriate practical support.

questions.question: What does it mean to confess Jesus as Lord?
options: Treat Jesus only as a moral teacher
Acknowledge Jesus’ authority and give him allegiance
Use a religious title without obedience
questions.explanation: Calling Jesus Lord is a confession of his authority that calls for faith, worship and obedience.
